description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, dark metal blade with a pronounced beard and a slightly curved cutting edge. The blade is attached to a wooden handle that displays a natural grain and a gentle ergonomic curve, likely designed for a comfortable grip. The handle appears to be reinforced near the head with a wrapped binding, possibly for added strength and durability. The axe head is broad and flat on one side, with a sharp, extended lower edge for chopping or splitting tasks. The wood of the handle shows some reddish-brown coloration, suggesting it is either stained or aged. The overall construction suggests a utilitarian tool suitable for heavy-duty use in urban maintenance, gardening, or emergency scenarios.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a dark metal blade and a curved wooden handle, reinforced with binding near the head. The blade has a pronounced beard and a sharp edge, making it ideal for chopping tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, dark metal blade with a pronounced beard.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the thickness of the blade and the ergonomic curvature of the handle, with the binding near the head visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's grain and the blade's profile, including the sharp edge and extended lower section.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the handle and the back of the axe head, with the binding and mounting details visible.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with visible grain, ergonomic shape, and reinforced with a fiber binding near the head; oval cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, dark steel blade with a pronounced beard, single sharp edge, and flat poll; utilitarian design with no visible decoration.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible fiber binding for reinforcement.
